mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high-payroll months fall below 0.5% despite PAYEMS > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in consumer price inflation (CPI) and increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals improved real purchasing power and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns in co-occurring CPI < 2% and HOUST > 700 periods fall below 1.2% or show negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (not directly observed) signals supply-side bottlenecks that increase corporate input costs and reduce equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO < 95 and CPIAUCSL > 2.5% periods exceed 1.5% or show positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A 10-year Treasury yield inversion (not directly observed) implied by a 200bp spread between FEDFUNDS and 10-year rates reduces equity risk premiums, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL > 200bp periods exceed -0.8% or show positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 8% combined with declining real sales (RSAFS) indicates weak consumer demand and corporate profit pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in UNRATE > 8% and RSAFS < 390000 periods exceed -0.5% or show positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.5%) signals strong economic expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1 > 3% and CPIAUCSL < 2.5% periods fall below 1.8% or show negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 96.5 followed by a 2%+ drop in oil prices (not directly observed) signals reduced manufacturing activity and deflationary pressure, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO < 96.5 and CPIAUCSL < 2% periods exceed -0.3% or show positive Rank IC.
